title: urban_bike_sharing
value: Generate a dataset for a city-wide bike sharing system operating 50,000 bicycles across 5,000 docking stations. Trip fact table with at most 1 million trips includes bike_id, user_id, start_station_id, end_station_id, start_time, end_time, trip_duration, and trip_distance. Dimension tables include station (location, capacity, neighborhood), bike (model, maintenance_status), and user (membership_type, signup_date). Model commuting patterns where morning flows move bikes from residential areas to business districts and evening flows reverse the pattern. Weather strongly influences demand: rain reduces trips by 60% while mild temperatures increase usage. Introduce rebalancing operations where trucks redistribute bikes between stations when capacity imbalances occur. Stations occasionally become empty or full, causing trip failures that users abandon or retry nearby. The dataset should stress spatio-temporal demand forecasting, station capacity planning, and operational optimization for bike redistribution.

title: food_delivery_platform
value: Create a dataset modeling a food delivery platform serving 2 million users and 100,000 restaurants. Order fact table with at most 1 million orders includes order_id, user_id, restaurant_id, courier_id, order_time, preparation_time, pickup_time, delivery_time, order_total, and tip_amount. Dimension tables for restaurant (cuisine_type, location, rating), courier (vehicle_type, shift_hours), and user (loyalty_level, signup_date). Demand peaks occur during lunch and dinner hours with strong weekend spikes. Delivery times depend on traffic conditions and restaurant preparation delays. Introduce batching where couriers deliver multiple nearby orders in a single trip. Restaurants occasionally pause orders during kitchen overload. Fraud scenarios include fake orders to inflate restaurant ratings or courier collusion. The dataset should stress route optimization, demand prediction, and anomaly detection in delivery operations.

title: airport_operations
value: Generate a dataset representing airport operations for 1,000 airports worldwide. Flight fact table with at most 1 million flights includes flight_id, airline_id, aircraft_id, departure_airport, arrival_airport, scheduled_departure, actual_departure, scheduled_arrival, actual_arrival, and delay_minutes. Dimension tables include airline, aircraft (model, capacity), airport (country, runway_count), and weather station. Delays propagate through airline networks when aircraft rotations are disrupted. Severe weather events cause correlated delays across nearby airports. Aircraft maintenance issues occasionally ground flights, requiring last-minute aircraft substitutions. Passenger load factors vary by route popularity and season. The dataset should stress network delay propagation analysis, schedule optimization, and disruption management.

title: online_learning_platform
value: Create a dataset for a global online learning platform with 10 million students and 50,000 courses. Enrollment fact table with at most 1 million enrollments includes student_id, course_id, enrollment_date, progress_percentage, completion_date, and final_score. Interaction fact table logs video views, quiz attempts, and assignment submissions. Dimension tables include course (category, difficulty_level), instructor (experience_years, institution), and student (country, education_level). Course engagement declines over time with most dropouts occurring in the first two weeks. High-rated instructors show higher completion rates. Live webinars create temporary spikes in engagement. Certificates are issued only for students achieving passing scores. The dataset should stress cohort analysis, learning path optimization, and engagement prediction.

title: smart_parking_system
value: Generate a dataset for a smart parking system monitoring 200,000 parking spaces across a metropolitan area. Parking event fact table with at most 1 million records includes space_id, vehicle_id, entry_time, exit_time, parking_duration, and fee_paid. Sensor table records occupancy status updates from ground sensors. Dimension tables include parking zone, pricing tier, and enforcement officer. Demand peaks during business hours in downtown areas and during events near stadiums. Dynamic pricing adjusts hourly based on occupancy rates. Illegal parking incidents trigger enforcement events with fines. Sensors occasionally fail or report noisy readings. The dataset should stress real-time occupancy monitoring, dynamic pricing optimization, and enforcement analytics.

title: electric_vehicle_charging
value: Create a dataset modeling electric vehicle charging networks with 100,000 charging stations and 5 million vehicles. Charging session fact table with at most 1 million sessions includes station_id, vehicle_id, session_start, session_end, energy_kwh, charging_cost, and payment_method. Dimension tables include station (location, charger_type, max_power), vehicle (battery_capacity, manufacturer), and operator. Charging demand peaks during evening hours and holidays when travel increases. Fast chargers experience higher utilization and occasional queuing. Grid load constraints cause operators to throttle charging speeds during peak demand. Some sessions terminate early due to user interruptions. The dataset should stress load balancing across charging networks, demand forecasting, and pricing strategies.

title: music_streaming_service
value: Generate a dataset for a music streaming service with 100 million users and 50 million tracks. Play event fact table with at most 1 million plays includes user_id, track_id, timestamp, device_type, playback_duration, and skip_flag. Dimension tables include artist, album, genre, and playlist. Popular songs follow a long-tail distribution where a few tracks receive massive plays while most receive very few. Viral trends create sudden spikes in plays for specific tracks. Users frequently discover music through playlists and recommendations. Some users exhibit binge-listening behavior during workouts or commuting. The dataset should stress recommendation systems, popularity trend detection, and user listening segmentation.

title: hospital_patient_flow
value: Create a dataset representing patient flow through a hospital with 2,000 beds and multiple departments. Admission fact table with at most 1 million admissions includes patient_id, admission_time, discharge_time, department_id, diagnosis_code, and treatment_cost. Transfer table tracks patient movements between departments such as emergency, ICU, and general wards. Dimension tables include doctor, department, and insurance provider. Emergency departments experience unpredictable surges during accidents or epidemics. ICU beds have limited capacity causing admission bottlenecks. Some patients require repeated readmissions due to chronic conditions. Treatment duration varies significantly depending on disease severity. The dataset should stress patient flow optimization, capacity planning, and readmission risk prediction.

title: construction_project_management
value: Generate a dataset for construction project management covering 10,000 large infrastructure projects. Task fact table with at most 1 million tasks includes task_id, project_id, contractor_id, start_date, end_date, planned_duration, actual_duration, and cost_overrun. Dimension tables include contractor, project_location, material_supplier, and equipment_type. Projects consist of multiple dependent tasks forming scheduling networks. Delays in early tasks propagate downstream causing project overruns. Weather conditions and supply chain disruptions affect material delivery schedules. Labor shortages occasionally slow construction progress. The dataset should stress critical path analysis, schedule risk modeling, and cost overrun prediction.

title: space_mission_operations
value: Create a dataset representing operations of multiple space missions including satellites and planetary probes. Telemetry fact table with at most 1 million readings includes spacecraft_id, timestamp, subsystem, metric_name, and metric_value. Event table logs maneuvers, instrument activations, and anomalies. Dimension tables include spacecraft (mission_type, launch_date), ground_station, and instrument payload. Telemetry values drift slowly over time due to component aging. Solar radiation events cause temporary spikes in error rates. Communication windows depend on spacecraft orbit and ground station visibility. Some missions experience anomalies requiring remote troubleshooting and command sequences. The dataset should stress anomaly detection in telemetry streams, mission operations scheduling, and subsystem reliability analysis.

title: space_debris_tracking
value: Generate a dataset for tracking orbital space debris around Earth using ground radar and satellite sensors. Observation fact table with at most 1 million records includes object_id, sensor_id, timestamp, altitude_km, velocity_kms, inclination_deg, and radar_cross_section. Dimension tables include debris_object (origin_mission, object_type, launch_year), sensor_station (country, radar_type, tracking_accuracy), and orbital_region (LEO, MEO, GEO). Objects occasionally collide creating fragmentation events that spawn many smaller debris pieces. Solar activity increases atmospheric drag causing low-orbit debris to decay faster. Space agencies perform avoidance maneuvers when predicted conjunction probability exceeds safety thresholds. The dataset should stress orbital trajectory prediction, collision risk detection, and long-term debris population modeling.

title: museum_collection_management
value: Create a dataset for managing collections across a global network of museums. Artifact fact table with at most 1 million artifacts includes artifact_id, collection_id, origin_country, historical_period, acquisition_date, and estimated_value. Exhibition fact table records artifact_id, exhibition_id, start_date, end_date, and display_location. Dimension tables include museum (city, country, founding_year), curator (specialization), and artifact_type (painting, sculpture, manuscript, artifact). Artifacts may be loaned between museums for temporary exhibitions. Conservation work occurs periodically and may temporarily remove artifacts from display. Popular exhibitions attract large visitor spikes. The dataset should stress artifact provenance tracking, exhibition scheduling, and cultural heritage analytics.

title: maritime_shipping_routes
value: Generate a dataset representing maritime shipping logistics for 50,000 cargo vessels across global trade routes. Voyage fact table with at most 1 million voyages includes vessel_id, origin_port, destination_port, departure_time, arrival_time, cargo_type, and cargo_weight. Dimension tables include vessel (capacity_tons, vessel_type), port (country, harbor_capacity), and shipping_company. Weather events such as storms may delay voyages and force route changes. Port congestion increases waiting times for docking. Fuel consumption varies with vessel speed and cargo weight. Trade demand fluctuates seasonally across regions. The dataset should stress route optimization, port congestion analysis, and maritime logistics forecasting.

title: video_game_multiplayer
value: Create a dataset for multiplayer online gaming sessions across millions of players. Match fact table with at most 1 million matches includes match_id, game_mode, map_id, start_time, end_time, and winner_team. Player match statistics table records player_id, match_id, kills, assists, deaths, damage_done, and score. Dimension tables include player (rank, region), weapon, map, and game_mode. Player skill ratings evolve over time based on match outcomes. New game updates may change weapon balance and affect player strategies. Some players form teams that consistently play together. The dataset should stress skill ranking algorithms, matchmaking optimization, and player behavior analytics.

title: public_library_network
value: Generate a dataset for a national public library system with thousands of branches. Loan fact table with at most 1 million book loans includes loan_id, book_id, member_id, branch_id, borrow_date, due_date, and return_date. Dimension tables include book (author, genre, publication_year), member (age_group, membership_type), and library_branch (city, capacity). Popular books frequently have waiting lists where members reserve copies. Late returns incur fines depending on membership type. Seasonal reading campaigns increase borrowing rates. Some books are transferred between branches to meet demand. The dataset should stress inventory balancing across branches, recommendation systems for readers, and demand forecasting for popular titles.

title: renewable_energy_farm
value: Create a dataset for monitoring renewable energy farms combining wind turbines and solar panels. Generation fact table with at most 1 million readings includes site_id, asset_id, timestamp, energy_output_kwh, wind_speed, solar_irradiance, and operational_status. Dimension tables include asset (type, capacity_kw, installation_date), site (region, grid_connection), and maintenance_team. Wind generation fluctuates with weather fronts while solar output follows daily cycles with cloud interruptions. Turbine failures reduce output until repaired by maintenance teams. Grid operators may curtail production during oversupply events. The dataset should stress renewable forecasting, asset reliability analysis, and grid integration optimization.

title: scientific_research_collaboration
value: Generate a dataset representing international scientific collaborations producing research papers. Publication fact table with at most 1 million papers includes paper_id, journal_id, publication_year, citation_count, and research_field. Authorship table records researcher_id, paper_id, and author_order. Dimension tables include researcher (institution, country, h_index), institution (type, ranking), and funding_agency. Research collaborations frequently span multiple countries and institutions. Citation counts follow a long-tail distribution where a few papers become highly influential. Funding agencies sponsor large research programs involving many teams. The dataset should stress co-authorship network analysis, citation impact modeling, and research collaboration patterns.

title: theme_park_operations
value: Create a dataset for operations of a large theme park with hundreds of attractions and millions of visitors. Ride usage fact table with at most 1 million records includes ride_id, visitor_id, queue_time_minutes, ride_start_time, ride_duration, and satisfaction_score. Dimension tables include ride (type, capacity, thrill_level), visitor (ticket_type, age_group), and park_zone. Visitor flow varies across the day with peak congestion around popular rides. Weather conditions affect outdoor attraction attendance. Some rides periodically close for maintenance causing queue redistribution. The dataset should stress visitor flow modeling, queue optimization, and ride popularity analytics.

title: waste_management_system
value: Generate a dataset for municipal waste management across a metropolitan region. Collection event fact table with at most 1 million records includes truck_id, route_id, collection_time, waste_type, and waste_weight_kg. Dimension tables include recycling_center, waste_processing_facility, truck, and neighborhood. Waste generation varies by neighborhood population density and seasonal events. Recycling rates differ by material type such as paper, plastic, and organic waste. Some facilities temporarily shut down for maintenance causing rerouting of waste trucks. Illegal dumping incidents occasionally occur and trigger inspections. The dataset should stress logistics route planning, waste recycling analytics, and environmental monitoring.

title: satellite_imagery_analysis
value: Create a dataset for satellite imagery processing pipelines used for earth observation. Image capture fact table with at most 1 million records includes satellite_id, capture_time, geographic_tile_id, cloud_coverage_percentage, and resolution_meters. Processing task table tracks image_id, processing_algorithm, processing_time, and output_product_type. Dimension tables include satellite (orbit_type, sensor_type), ground_station, and geographic_region. Cloud cover may render some images unusable. Different algorithms produce outputs such as vegetation indices, urban growth maps, or disaster damage assessments. Satellite revisit times determine how frequently a region is imaged. The dataset should stress large-scale geospatial analytics, remote sensing pipelines, and environmental monitoring applications.
